refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall update the worksite model within the customer-required timeliness for operational use.
refined_fr.rationale: The candidate FR specifies a single functional obligation: timely updating of the worksite model according to customer requirements. It does not combine multiple functions or include implementation details, thus it is atomic and solution-neutral.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure the latency of worksite model updates under supported scenarios and verify that the 95th percentile latency (p95) meets or is below the customer-defined target (e.g., ≤ 1.0 s).
sub_frs: The NGHE solution shall update the worksite model within the customer-required timeliness for operational use.

candidate_dps: Incremental model update mechanism triggered by detected changes
Periodic full model refresh at fixed intervals
Event-driven update triggered by external signals or sensor inputs
Hybrid approach combining incremental updates with periodic full refreshes
Prioritized update focusing on critical model elements first
selected_dp.selected_dp: Incremental model update mechanism triggered by detected changes

coupling_check.notes: The incremental update mechanism logically isolates update triggers and processing, minimizing dependencies between unrelated model components and supporting timely updates.

assumptions_risks: Assumes that changes can be detected reliably and quickly to trigger updates
Risk that incremental updates may accumulate errors or inconsistencies over time without periodic full refresh
Assumes that the update mechanism can meet the p95 latency target under expected load and complexity
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider explicitly including a fallback or periodic full refresh DP to mitigate risks of incremental update drift and ensure model consistency over time.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution module encompasses the core system logic responsible for managing model updates and integration with other system components.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives data inputs that may trigger model updates.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Sends updated model tasks to other modules.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Facilitates communication for update propagation and synchronization.

module_containment_scores.notes: High cohesion as the incremental model update mechanism is central to NGHE Solution; minimal diffusion outside this module.
Partial containment possible but model update logic would diffuse significantly to other modules.
Low containment; teleoperation focuses on control rather than model updates.
Very low containment; safety services are peripheral to model update logic.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The incremental model update mechanism is best contained within the NGHE Solution module, which centralizes core system logic and minimizes diffusion across other modules.
containment_risks: Potential coupling with Vehicle module if updates require real-time vehicle state data.
Dependency on Network Link interface may introduce latency or synchronization issues.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP 'Incremental model update mechanism triggered by detected changes' directly addresses the FR of updating the worksite model within the required timeliness by focusing on incremental updates triggered by changes, which supports meeting the latency acceptance criteria.

diffusion_risks: Potential coupling with Vehicle module if updates require real-time vehicle state data.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The NGHE Solution module shows high cohesion with a containment score of 8 and low diffusion score of 2, indicating the incremental model update mechanism is well contained. Minimal diffusion outside this module reduces risk of scattered responsibilities.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible by measuring the worksite model update latency (p95 ≤ 1.0 s) through the NGHE Solution module interfaces, especially via Telemetry input and Tasking output.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The ownership candidate 'NGHE Solution' clearly encapsulates the DP within a single module boundary, providing clear allocation and minimizing ambiguity.

recommended_revisions: Monitor and mitigate coupling risks with the Vehicle module to prevent diffusion of update logic.
Assess and address potential latency or synchronization issues arising from dependency on the Network Link interface to ensure timeliness requirements are met.

decision.justification: All gates (FR->DP, DP->IM, FR->IM) have passed successfully, indicating that the current functional requirement is adequately addressed by the design parameter and implementation module. The incremental model update mechanism is well contained within the NGHE Solution module, with clear ownership and feasible verification methods. Although there are some coupling risks with the Vehicle module and potential latency issues via the Network Link interface, these are noted as monitoring and mitigation actions rather than blockers for further decomposition. Given the high safety criticality, maintaining clarity and containment at this level is preferable to avoid unnecessary complexity.

revise.why: Monitor and mitigate coupling risks with the Vehicle module and assess latency/synchronization issues related to the Network Link interface to ensure timeliness and containment are maintained.

assumptions: The incremental update mechanism sufficiently meets the latency acceptance criteria (p95 ≤ 1.0 s).
The NGHE Solution module ownership is stable and well-defined.
Coupling with the Vehicle module can be managed through monitoring and mitigation strategies.
open_risks: Potential coupling with Vehicle module causing diffusion of update logic.
Latency or synchronization issues arising from dependency on Network Link interface.
evidence_needed: Performance measurements of worksite model update latency under operational conditions.
Verification results of interface interactions between NGHE Solution and Vehicle modules.
Monitoring reports on coupling and diffusion risks over time.
